data.title: SONIM TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Sonim Technologies Inc., a leading provider of enterprise 5G solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting its ongoing efforts to expand its product portfolio and market reach in the rugged mobile device industry.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Revenues: $11.2 million, a decrease of 3% compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to a decline in sales of legacy phones.

Gross Profit: $0.8 million, a significant decrease of 72% compared to 2024, mainly attributed to the impairment of contract fulfillment assets and additional tariff costs.

Income (loss) from operations: $(6.7) million, representing a slight increase in loss by 4% compared to 2024.

Net income (loss): $(7.5) million, an increased loss by 13% compared to 2024.

Business Highlights

Company Overview: Sonim Technologies is a leading provider of enterprise 5G solutions, offering rugged handsets, smartphones, wireless internet devices, software, services, and accessories. The company serves sectors such as critical communications, first responders, government, industrial, construction, hospitality, and logistics.

Geographical Performance: Sonim's products are available through major carriers in the U.S., Canada, and Australia, and are expanding in Europe and South Africa through new distribution partners. This expansion supports the company's commitment to offering reliable solutions and expanding its customer base.

New Product Launches: In the second half of 2024 and through the filing date of this report, Sonim launched several new products, including the Sonim H500-series of 5G mobile hotspots, Sonim MegaConnect, Sonim H100 4G mobile hotspot, XP100 4G and XP400 5G professional rugged phones, XP Pro 5G rugged smartphone, XP Pro Thermal 5G rugged smartphone, and XP3plus 5G rugged flip phone.

Product Features: The new products boast features such as IP ratings, MIL-STD-810H standards, and elements of Sonim’s RPS, highlighting the company's value proposition to target markets.

Future Outlook: Sonim is focused on expanding its product portfolio throughout 2025, with plans to launch the XP Pro Thermal 5G smartphone in Europe, featuring an SDK-enabled Sonim IRIS software and an integrated thermal camera by FLIR®.

Revenue Segments: For the six months ended June 30, 2025, rugged smartphones represented 57% of total net revenues, feature phones were 31%, and connected solutions were 11%. For the three months ended June 30, 2025, rugged smartphones represented 43% of total net revenues, feature phones were 34%, and connected solutions were 20%.

Customer Base: The company's customer base is highly concentrated, with wireless carriers contributing 86% of total net revenues for the six months ended June 30, 2025, and the top three carrier customers accounting for 74% of total net revenues.

Trade Agreements Act Compliance: Sonim has prioritized its Trade Agreements Act initiatives to enhance market strategy and expand opportunities within government and enterprise markets, reinforcing its position as a trusted provider of enterprise 5G solutions.

SEC Filing: SONIM TECHNOLOGIES INC [ SONM ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: RAYONIER INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Rayonier Inc., a leading timberland real estate investment trust (REIT), has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by strategic divestitures and a focus on core operations in the Southern and Pacific Northwest regions.

Financial Highlights

Rayonier Inc. reported robust financial results for the three months ended June 30, 2025, showcasing substantial growth across key metrics:

  • Sales: $106.5 million, up from $99.6 million in the same period of 2024.
  • Operating Income: $14.5 million, a significant increase from $4.5 million in the same period of 2024, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $413.6 million, a dramatic rise from $3.0 million in the same period of 2024, primarily due to the gain on the sale of discontinued operations.
  • Net Income Attributable to Rayonier Inc.: $408.7 million, compared to $1.9 million in the same period of 2024.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share Attributable to Rayonier Inc.: $2.63, compared to $0.01 in the same period of 2024.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share Attributable to Rayonier Inc.: $2.63, compared to $0.01 in the same period of 2024.

Business Highlights

Rayonier Inc. also reported notable operational achievements and strategic shifts during the second quarter of 2025:

  • Revenue Segments: The company generated significant revenue from its Southern Timber and Pacific Northwest Timber segments. Southern Timber contributed $43.8 million, while Pacific Northwest Timber added $21.2 million to the total timber sales for the three months ended June 30, 2025.
  • Geographical Performance: The company completed the sale of its New Zealand operations, which were previously a significant part of its geographical revenue. These operations are now classified as discontinued, impacting the geographical revenue distribution.
  • Sales Units: The Southern Timber segment reported sales of 19,132 units under Stumpage Pay-as-Cut contracts and 24,631 units under Delivered Wood (Domestic) contracts. The Pacific Northwest Timber segment reported 561 units under Stumpage Lump Sum contracts and 20,601 units under Delivered Wood (Domestic) contracts for the three months ended June 30, 2025.
  • Future Outlook: Following the divestiture of its New Zealand operations, Rayonier Inc. is focusing on optimizing its core operations in the Southern and Pacific Northwest regions. This strategic shift is expected to streamline operations and enhance focus on domestic markets.

SEC Filing: RAYONIER INC [ RYN ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Ares Management Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Ares Management Corp, a leading global alternative investment manager, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational achievements, reflecting the company's robust performance and strategic growth initiatives. Below is a detailed summary of the key financial and business highlights from the report.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $1,350.1 million, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year driven by higher management fees and carried interest allocation.
  • Net Income: $225.98 million, a decrease compared to the previous year, impacted by increased compensation and benefits expenses.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.46, consistent with the previous year, indicating stable earnings per share despite fluctuations in net income.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.46, reflecting the same value as basic earnings per share, indicating no significant dilution effect from potential share conversions.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported significant growth in management fees, carried interest allocation, and administrative, transaction, and other fees. Management fees increased to $900,622 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $721,681 for the same period in 2024. Carried interest allocation also saw a substantial increase, reaching $323,901 from a negative $51,167 in the previous year.
  • Geographical Performance: The acquisition of GCP International expanded the company's geographic presence, particularly enhancing its capabilities in real estate and digital infrastructure investments. This acquisition is expected to contribute to the company's growth in international markets.
  • New Product Launches: The acquisition of GCP International added complementary real estate and digital infrastructure investment capabilities, which are expected to enhance the company's product offerings and market reach.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in its Real Assets Group segment, driven by the integration of GCP International and the expansion of its investment capabilities. The management expects the acquisition to generate synergies and enhance the company's competitive position in the global market.

SEC Filing: Ares Management Corp [ ARES ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Youxin Technology Offering Up to 14.5M Units Consisting of Stock and Warrants
TV broadcaster Nexstar in advanced talks to acquire rival Tegna, WSJ reports
data.description:
Television broadcaster Nexstar Media Group is in advanced talks to acquire rival Tegna , the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Shares of Tegna surged 30% in extended trading following the report, while Nexstar's shares remained flat.

A potential deal between the two companies would mark a significant step in the ongoing consolidation of the U.S. television industry, as broadcasters adapt to shifting consumer habits driven by cord-cutting and the rapid expansion of streaming, amid expectations of looser regulations under U.S. President Donald Trump's administration.

Nexstar, owns or partners with over 200 stations in 116 markets. It also operates high-profile media properties including The CW and NewsNation, and has recently increased its focus on sports content.

Tegna owns 64 stations and operates networks such as True Crime Network. The company has a market valuation of approximately $2.42 billion, compared with Nexstar’s $5.56 billion, according to LSEG data.

A deal could materialize soon, provided the talks do not encounter last-minute snags, according to the report

Tegna has been subject to takeover interest in the past. In 2022, it agreed to be taken private by Standard General in a deal valued at $8.6 billion, including debt, but later terminated the merger agreement following regulatory scrutiny.

Nexstar declined to comment. Tegna did not immediately respond to Reuters’ request for comment.

data.title: Harvard patents targeted by Trump administration
data.description:
Key points:
  • Harvard allegedly breaches legal, contractual obligations
  • US threatens to take ownership of patents or grant licenses
  • Harvard not available for comment

The Trump administration on Friday ordered a comprehensive review of Harvard University's federally funded research programs, and threatened to take title to or grant licenses from the school's lucrative portfolio of patents.

In a letter to University President Alan Garber and obtained by Reuters, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick accused Harvard of breaching its legal and contractual requirements tied to the research programs and patents.

Lutnick also said the Commerce Department has begun a "march-in" process under the federal Bayh-Dole Act that could let the government take ownership of or grant licenses to Harvard's patents.

That law ensures that Americans benefit from inventions funded through taxpayer dollars, a senior administration official said.

The letter demands that Harvard provide by September 5 a list of all patents stemming from federally funded research grants, including how the patents are used and whether any licensing requires "substantial U.S. manufacturing."

Harvard did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

As of July 1, 2024, Harvard held more than 5,800 patents, and had more than 900 technology licenses with over 650 industry partners, according to a university website.

President Donald Trump has made bolstering the country's manufacturing and economic competitiveness a priority of his second White House term, including by raising tariffs on dozens of countries.

The letter ratchets up the White House's pressure on Harvard, which it has accused of civil rights violations for failing to address antisemitism on campus.

Harvard sued in April after the administration began stripping or freezing billions of dollars of federal research money.

Other schools that have faced funding losses have included Columbia University, which last month agreed to pay more than $220 million to settle the government's antisemitism claims.

The New York Times has said Harvard was open to spending up to $500 million to settle similar claims.

Many civil rights experts, faculty and White House critics believe the administration's targeting of schools is a pretext to assert federal control and threaten academic freedom and free speech.

data.title: Prosecutors add allegations against poker-playing Supreme Court lawyer Goldstein
data.description:
Tom Goldstein funneled nearly $1 million of his and his wife's money into a trust account controlled by his law firm in order to shield it from the Internal Revenue Service, federal prosecutors alleged in an expanded indictment against the prominent former U.S. Supreme Court lawyer.

The superseding indictment, filed in federal court in Greenbelt, Maryland, on Thursday, also alleged Goldstein in 2020 and 2021 offered cryptocurrency, student loan payments and a $10,000 bonus to a manager at his former firm Goldstein & Russell "in part to dissuade the firm manager from cooperating with the IRS's ongoing criminal investigation."

Goldstein, who argued more than 40 cases at the Supreme Court before retiring in 2023 and who co-founded the SCOTUSblog Supreme Court news website, was first indicted in January on 22 counts of tax evasion and other financial criminal charges allegedly connected to his side career as a high-stakes poker player.

Goldstein has pleaded not guilty. The new indictment brings additional allegations and details to the case but does not include new charges. Goldstein's spokesperson and his defense lawyers at Munger, Tolles & Olson did not immediately respond to requests for comment on the new allegations.

A spokesperson for the Maryland U.S. Attorney's Office did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Federal prosecutors have said Goldstein won and lost millions of dollars in individual poker matches and made improper payments through his law firm to cover debts.

The superseding indictment said Goldstein transferred $960,000 into Goldstein & Russell's IOLTA account, a type of trust account that holds funds for clients, in order to avoid an IRS levy.

He then moved the funds to his firm's business account before using it to buy a new house in Washington, D.C., in March 2021, prosecutors alleged.

data.title: Coastal, offshore grades broadly rise at WTI/Brent spread widens
data.description:
Coastal and offshore grades broadly rose on Friday, dealers said, as the spread between U.S. crude futures and Brent widened.

U.S. crude futures' discount to Brent widened to as much as minus $3.61, its most since mid-June. A wider spread makes U.S. grades more attractive to foreign buyers.

U.S. energy firms this week cut the number of oil and natural gas rigs operating for a third week in a row, energy services firm Baker Hughes said in its closely followed report on Friday.

* Light Louisiana Sweet (WTC-LLS) for September delivery was steady at a midpoint of a $1.93 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $1.70 and $2.15 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* Mars Sour (WTC-MRS) rose 15 cents to a midpoint of a 15-cent premium and was seen bid and offered between a 5-cent and 25-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* WTI Midland (WTC-WTM) rose 5 cents to a midpoint of a 45-cent premium and was seen bid and offered between a 35-cent and 55-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* West Texas Sour (WTC-WTS) fell 2 cents to a midpoint of a 20-cent discount and was seen bid and offered between a 25-cent and 15-cent a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures

* WTI at East Houston (WTC-MEH), also known as MEH, traded between a 65-cent and 85-cent a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

* ICE Brent October futures rose 16 cents to settle at $66.59 a barrel

* WTI September crude futures remained unchanged at $63.88 a barrel

* The Brent/WTI spread (WTCLc1-LCOc1) widened 22 cents to last trade at minus $3.61, after hitting a high of minus $3.38 and a low of minus $3.61

data.title: Materials Shares Rise as Gold Futures Rally, Then Retreat — Materials Roundup
data.description:
Materials shares rose on news that Trump was leaving tariffs on gold, sending prices to all-time highs. The sector held its ground even after the White House said there was misinformation in reports about the new tariffs.

Fortescue's new $2 billion yuan-denominated loan is roughly four times what the iron-ore miner initially sought from lenders, reflecting strong demand among Chinese banks to put cash toward decarbonization.

Expectations of interest rate cuts in the U.S. were also supporting gold futures.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Industrial Shares Fall Slightly — Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies fell slightly as the sector grappled with lingering uncertainty about tariffs.

U.K. antitrust officials said they wouldn't open an in-depth probe into Boeing's deal to acquire fuselage maker Spirit AeroSystems Holdings, effectively clearing the transaction weeks after they launched the first phase of an investigation.

Uber shares fell after the company announced a partnership with Dollar General to offer the discount retailer's products on its delivery platform.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Correction: BlueNord ASA - Award of Performance Shares under LTI-Program
data.description:
BlueNord ASA

Oslo, 8 August 2024: Reference is made to the previous stock exchange notices regarding grant and award of performance shares under the Long-Term Incentive Scheme (“LTI”) of BlueNord ASA (“BlueNord” or the “Company”) as first announced on 19 September 2022. Further reference is made to BlueNord’s announcements, including on 14 July 2025, regarding repurchase of own shares for the purpose of, amongst other things, use in the Company’s share incentive program.

The third and final conclusion of the award of performance shares under the LTI program established in 2022 has been made today, and the Company’s board of directors has resolved to deliver a total of 25,124* existing shares in the Company to the participants in the 2022 LTI program. The number of vested shares applicable for the award constitutes 50% of the total number of granted performance shares as announced by the Company on 19 September 2022 to each of the primary insiders. The award is adjusted for the KPI performance applicable for the 2022 LTI program and the shares are awarded net of tax based on the share price of NOK 477.50 at the time of award.

Following the delivery of these shares, the Company owns 976,658 own shares in treasury.

Following this award, the total number of shares that maximum can be awarded under the LTI program is 122,806 (before tax). 

The following members of the Executive Management of the Company have today been allocated the following number of shares under the LTI program:

  • Euan Shirlaw, Chief Executive Officer, was awarded 6,465 shares. Following this award, Euan Shirlaw owns a total of 13,561 shares in the Company. In addition, Shirlaw has the right to receive maximum up to 23,229 shares in the Company under the LTI program and 60,014 retention shares in the Company vesting in September 2025, in each case before tax.
  • Jacqueline Lindmark Boye, Chief Financial Officer, was awarded 785 shares. Following this award, Jacqueline Lindmark Boye owns a total of 1,740 shares in the Company. In addition, Boye has the right to receive maximum up to 12,042 shares in the Company under the LTI program before tax.
  • Miriam Lykke, Chief Operating Officer, was awarded 1,285 shares. Following this award, Miriam Lykke owns a total of 2,292 shares in the Company. In addition, Lykke has the right to receive maximum up to 5,372 shares in the Company under the LTI program before tax.
  • Cathrine Torgersen, Chief Corporate Affairs Officer, was awarded 3,270 shares. Following this award, Cathrine Torgersen owns a total of 8,985 shares in the Company. In addition, Torgersen has the right to receive maximum up to 10,435 shares in the Company under the LTI program and 20,404 retention shares in the Company vesting in September 2025, in each case before tax.

Future grants and awards of shares made under the Company’s LTI programs shall be announced if and when made.

Please see attached primary insider notification forms in accordance with the Market Abuse Regulation article 19.

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to Section 5-12 the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

***

Contact:

Cathrine Torgersen, Chief Corporate Affairs Officer

Phone: +47 915 28 501

Email: cathrine.torgersen@bluenord.com

About BlueNord ASA

BlueNord is a strategically important European oil and gas company that specialises in producing and developing energy resources, and in activities which support the energy transition. The Company operates in the Danish North Sea with a 36.8 percent interest in the Danish Underground Consortium (DUC). BlueNord is listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange and trades under the ticker "BNOR". For further information, please visit: www.bluenord.

data.title: Correction: BlueNord ASA - Award of Performance Shares under LTI-Program
data.description:
Oslo, 8 August 2024: Reference is made to the previous stock exchange noticesregarding grant and award of performance shares under the Long-Term IncentiveScheme ("LTI") of BlueNord ASA ("BlueNord" or the "Company") as firstannouncedon 19 September 2022. Further reference is made to BlueNord's announcements,including on 14 July 2025, regarding repurchase of own shares for the purposeof, amongst other things, use in the Company's share incentive program.

The third and final conclusion of the award of performance shares under theLTIprogram established in 2022 has been made today, and the Company's board ofdirectors has resolved to deliver a total of 25,124* existing shares in theCompany to the participants in the 2022 LTI program. The number of vestedsharesapplicable for the award constitutes 50% of the total number of grantedperformance shares as announced by the Company on 19 September 2022 to each ofthe primary insiders. The award is adjusted for the KPI performance applicablefor the 2022 LTI program and the shares are awarded net of tax based on theshare price of NOK 477.50 at the time of award.

Following the delivery of these shares, the Company owns 976,658 own shares intreasury.

Following this award, the total number of shares that maximum can be awardedunder the LTI program is 122,806 (before tax).

The following members of the Executive Management of the Company have todaybeenallocated the following number of shares under the LTI program:

· Euan Shirlaw, Chief Executive Officer, was awarded 6,465 shares. Followingthis award, Euan Shirlaw owns a total of 13,561 shares in the Company. Inaddition, Shirlaw has the right to receive maximum up to 23,229 shares in theCompany under the LTI program and 60,014 retention shares in the Companyvestingin September 2025, in each case before tax. · Jacqueline Lindmark Boye, Chief Financial Officer, was awarded 785 shares.Following this award, Jacqueline Lindmark Boye owns a total of 1,740 shares inthe Company. In addition, Boye has the right to receive maximum up to 12,042shares in the Company under the LTI program before tax. · Miriam Lykke, Chief Operating Officer, was awarded 1,285 shares. Followingthis award, Miriam Lykke owns a total of 2,292 shares in the Company. Inaddition, Lykke has the right to receive maximum up to 5,372 shares in theCompany under the LTI program before tax. · Cathrine Torgersen, Chief Corporate Affairs Officer, was awarded 3,270shares. Following this award, Cathrine Torgersen owns a total of 8,985 sharesinthe Company. In addition, Torgersen has the right to receive maximum up to10,435 shares in the Company under the LTI program and 20,404 retention sharesin the Company vesting in September 2025, in each case before tax.

Future grants and awards of shares made under the Company's LTI programs shallbe announced if and when made.

data.title: Sonim Technologies' Q2 revenue falls 33%
data.description:
Overview

  • Sonim Q2 revenue falls 33% sequentially, reflecting absence of one-time boost

  • Net loss for Q2 was $7.5 mln, compared to Q1 net income

Outlook

  • Sonim expects tier-one carrier launches to drive growth in H2 2025

  • Company focuses on leveraging resources for growth and innovation

Result Drivers

  • PRODUCT LAUNCHES - Sonim launched the XP Pro smartphone with AT&T and began shipments of the H500 mobile hotspot to European distributors

  • IMPAIRMENT COSTS - Loss on impairment of contract fulfillment assets related to legacy products impacted financial results

  • LEGAL EXPENSES - Increased legal and professional fees due to contested proxy solicitation contributed to higher operating expenses

Key Details

data.title: S&P/TSX Composite Index Ends the Week 2.73% Higher at 27758.68 — Data Talk
data.description:
The S&P/TSX Composite Index is up 738.25 points or 2.73% this week to 27758.68

  • Largest one-week point and percentage gain since the week ending Sept. 13, 2024
  • Up 14 of the past 18 weeks
  • Today it is down 2.59 points or 0.01%
  • Third highest close in history
  • Down for two consecutive trading days
  • Down 162.19 points or 0.58% over the last two trading days
  • Largest two-day point and percentage decline since Friday, Aug. 1, 2025
  • Down five of the past seven trading days
  • Off 0.58% from its record close of 27920.87 hit Wednesday, Aug. 6, 2025
  • Up 10.73% from the Inauguration Day close of 25067.92 on Monday, Jan 20, 2025
  • Today's closing value is the third highest this year
  • Off 0.58% from its 52-week high of 27920.87 hit Wednesday, Aug. 6, 2025
  • Up 24.42% from its 52-week low of 22311.30 hit Friday, Aug. 9, 2024
  • Rose 24.42% from 52 weeks ago
  • Off 0.58% from its 2025 closing high of 27920.87 hit Wednesday, Aug. 6, 2025
  • Up 23.33% from its 2025 closing low of 22506.90 hit Tuesday, April 8, 2025
  • Month-to-date it is up 1.83%
  • Year-to-date it is up 3030.74 points or 12.26%

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: Health Care Stocks Rise Amid Wall Street Bullishness — Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Stocks in the health-care industry rose as markets grew more confident that U.S. borrowing costs will fall, while companies in the sector pleased investors in their quarterly reports.

Gilead Sciences stock rose after the biopharmaceutical company raised its guidance, citing strong demand for its HIV medicine and solid quarterly earnings.

Artivion shares rose after the company's second quarter profit beat expectations and full-year guidance improved.

GSK said it would receive $370 million as part of a U.S. patent settlement between CureVac and BioNTech related to messenger mRNA-based Covid-19 vaccines.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com
